title: Portfolio Website
short_description: A responsive Django portfolio with file-based content, contact inbox, resume uploads, and production deployment.
description:
A personal portfolio website built with Django using the MVT architecture. The project now uses a file-based content layer for portfolio pages, so projects, skills, certifications, achievements, and education can be updated through JSON files without pushing that content into database tables.

Key backend features:
- File-based content loading for portfolio sections.
- Django templates for server-rendered pages.
- Contact form with validation, spam protection, email delivery, and database-backed message storage.
- Resume download endpoint using Django FileResponse.
- Admin panel kept only for contact messages, resume uploads, and Django user management.
- Static asset delivery through WhiteNoise for Vercel deployment.
- Environment-based configuration for secrets, database, email, and production security.

Why it matters:
This project demonstrates a practical hybrid Django architecture: static portfolio content stays version-controlled in external files, while truly dynamic operational data remains in the database.

title: Heart Disease Prediction
short_description: A machine learning project for predicting heart disease risk from health-related input features.
description:
A machine learning project that predicts heart disease risk from user-provided health parameters. The project is useful for explaining the complete ML workflow: data preprocessing, feature selection, model training, evaluation, and exposing predictions through a simple application interface.

Backend and ML workflow:
- Clean and prepare tabular health data.
- Train a supervised classification model using Python ML libraries.
- Accept user input, transform it into the model-ready format, and return a prediction.
- Deploy the app so predictions can be tested from a browser.

Interview angle:
The important part is not only the prediction result, but the workflow: understand the dataset, choose features, train a model, evaluate it honestly, and integrate it with an application users can interact with.

title: House Price Prediction
short_description: A regression-based ML project for estimating property prices from housing features.
description:
A machine learning regression project that estimates house prices from structured housing data. The project highlights data cleaning, feature engineering, training regression models, and interpreting which features influence the predicted price.

Core workflow:
- Load and clean housing data.
- Handle missing values and encode usable features.
- Train and compare regression models.
- Evaluate predictions using regression metrics.
- Present the result in a simple, explainable way.

Interview angle:
This project is a good example for discussing supervised learning, regression metrics, and why model interpretation is important when predictions affect real-world decisions.

title: Expense Tracker
short_description: A personal finance web app for recording expenses and reviewing spending categories.
description:
A web application for tracking personal expenses and organizing spending by category. It is useful for explaining CRUD operations, database-backed records, form validation, and dashboard-style summaries.

Core features:
- Add, update, and delete expense records.
- Categorize transactions for easier review.
- Show basic summaries that help users understand spending patterns.
- Keep the interface simple enough for repeated daily use.

Interview angle:
This project is a clean way to discuss backend CRUD design, validation, database tables, and how user-facing dashboards are powered by aggregated data.

title: Food Analyzer AI
short_description: An in-progress AI project for food image analysis and nutrition-focused insights.
description:
An in-progress AI project focused on analyzing food images and connecting image recognition with nutrition-related insights.

Planned workflow:
- Collect and organize food image data.
- Train or fine-tune an image classification model.
- Return likely food labels from uploaded images.
- Connect predictions with nutrition information.
- Build a user interface for uploading images and reviewing results.

Interview angle:
Because this project is still in progress, it is best to present it honestly as a learning project. Explain the planned pipeline, current challenges, and what you would improve next.

title: Google Opal AI Workflow App
short_description: An AI-powered workflow app built using Google Opal for experimenting with prompt-based automation.
description:
An AI workflow application created using Google Opal to experiment with no-code and low-code AI app building. The project focuses on designing an AI-powered flow where user input is processed through structured prompts and connected steps to generate useful output.

Core features:
- Built using Google Opal's visual AI app-building environment.
- Designed a prompt-based workflow for handling user input and producing AI-generated responses.
- Used connected blocks and steps to organize the flow logically.
- Helps demonstrate understanding of AI workflow design and prompt engineering.
- Useful for quickly prototyping AI-based tools without building a full backend from scratch.

Interview angle:
This project is useful to explain prompt engineering, AI workflow design, rapid prototyping, and how no-code AI tools can be used to test product ideas before building a full custom application.

title: Dental Clinic AI Chatbot
short_description: An AI receptionist chatbot for dental appointment booking using Retell AI and n8n automation.
description:
An AI-powered dental clinic receptionist system designed to handle patient calls and automate appointment-related tasks. The chatbot acts as a virtual receptionist and helps users with booking, rescheduling, cancellation, and general clinic inquiries.

Core features:
- Handles patient conversations using a voice-based AI receptionist.
- Checks appointment availability using Google Calendar.
- Books dental appointments and stores patient details.
- Supports rescheduling and cancellation flows.
- Logs appointment data and call summaries into Google Sheets.
- Handles transfer call and end-call flows through automation.
- Uses short, clear responses suitable for voice assistant latency.

Interview angle:
This project is strong for explaining AI automation, voice agents, webhook-based backend workflows, n8n automation, API integration, Google Calendar scheduling, Google Sheets logging, and real-world business process automation.

title: Sanskrit Character Recognition
short_description: A FastAPI-based ML and CNN project for recognizing Sanskrit characters and word images.
description:
A machine learning and deep learning based recognition system for uploaded Sanskrit character and word images. The project provides a web interface where users can upload images and get predictions from trained models.

Core features:
- Upload Sanskrit character or word images for prediction.
- FastAPI backend exposes prediction and evaluation APIs.
- Frontend is built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
- Supports multiple model approaches including Pixel MLP, CNN embeddings with Linear SVM, and an end-to-end CNN model.
- Returns unclassified output for blank, low-quality, ambiguous, or out-of-dataset inputs.
- Includes model evaluation endpoints for checking model performance.

Interview angle:
This project is useful to explain computer vision basics, image preprocessing, ML vs CNN model comparison, FastAPI model serving, API-based prediction flow, and how trained ML models are integrated into a web application.

title: Directory Automation File Sorter
short_description: A Python GUI tool that automatically organizes files into folders based on their extensions.
description:
A desktop automation tool built in Python to organize files inside a selected directory based on their file extensions. The project includes a simple GUI so users can select a folder, preview sorting actions, and then perform the actual organization.

Core features:
- Select a directory using a GUI folder picker.
- Automatically sort files into folders based on file extensions.
- Dry run mode to preview changes before moving files.
- Real-time log viewer to show sorting actions.
- Option to save logs into timestamped text files.
- Uses only Python standard library, making it lightweight and easy to run.

Interview angle:
This project is useful to explain Python automation, file handling, GUI development, OS module usage, safe execution using dry-run mode, logging, and how small automation tools can improve daily productivity.
